item.title: Gears of War: E-Day - Open Beta Start Dates and How To Get Early Access
item.content:encoded:
Gears of War: E-Day's big showing at the Xbox Games Showcase was primarily focused on its campaign, but the game is also set to have what will undoubtedly be a killer multiplayer mode too, and players won't have to wait too long to try it.

Xbox has confirmed that a multiplayer beta is coming, starting on August 6. While the "open beta" will eventually be available to everyone, those who preorder Gears of War: E-Day can get in early and play during the first beta weekend.

Here are all the details you need until then.

Gears of War: E-Day

As far as Beta Codes go, the Gears website also says that, "Players who digitally pre-order Gears of War: E-Day are automatically granted Early Access to the Open Beta and do not require a Beta Code."

Players who pre-order a physical edition should expect to receive a Beta Code at the point of sale from participating retailers, granting entry into Open Beta for Early Access."

That means Gears of War: E-Day's beta is ultimately open to everyone, but, as we mentioned, players who preorder any edition of the game can jump in before the wider public (that includes the standard, premium, or collector's edition).

Xbox Game Pass and PC Game Pass subscribers will also receive access during the August 6 weekend as part of their membership benefits.

As far as how it works for everyone else looking to access in the second beta weekend, we'll have to await further information from Xbox.

When is the Gears of War: E-Day Open Beta?

Xbox has confirmed that Gears of War: E-Day's multiplayer beta will be split across two weekends, starting on August 6.

Gears of War E-Day is an Xbox Console Exclusive

Standard Edition of Gears of War: E-Day is available in both a physical and digital format, and listed for $69.99. But, it's only available for Xbox consoles and on PC. This one isn't coming to PS5.

Alongside the main game and getting early access to the Open Beta, ordering now also sets you up with a preorder bonus of a character skin and weapon skin.

Gears of War: E-Day Gameplay Demo Reveal Xbox Games Showcase 2026 - Screenshots

If you are going this route, I'd suggest a choosing retailer like Best Buy or Amazon, which won't charge you until the game eventually ships in October later this year.

If you don't click with the game during the beta, you can simply cancel your preorder without the stress of having to await a refund.

Robert Anderson is IGN's Senior Commerce Editor and resident deals expert on games, collectibles, trading card games, and more. You can follow him @robertliam21 on Bluesky.



https://www.ign.com/articles/gears-of-war-e-day-open-beta-how-to-play
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorders Are Live for PS5 and Xbox – Here’s What Comes in Each Edition
item.content:encoded:
Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorders Are Live for PS5 and Xbox – Here’s What Comes in Each Edition

Releasing very soon on July 28.

Good news, Halo fans: Halo: Campaign Evolved will be released next month on July 28. If you simply can't wait to spend your summer days absorbed in this remake, preorders are already live, so you can secure a copy today.

A Standard Edition, Premium Edition, and Collector's Edition are all available to check out right now, and it's not just Xbox users that get to drop some cash on this release. The game is also coming to PlayStation 5, alongside PC, so everyone can step into Master Chief’s shoes this summer.

Below you can learn more about each edition, what they're priced at, and what they come with.

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Standard Edition

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Premium Edition

Halo: Campaign Evolved Premium Edition - Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Windows [Digital]

Digital

  • See at Best Buy (Xbox Series X/S) - $69.99
  • See at Xbox - $69.99
  • See at PlayStation - $69.99

PC

  • See at Steam - $69.99

Halo: Campaign Evolved's Premium Edition, which appears to only be available as a digital version, is priced at $69.99. This comes with quite a few bonuses alongside the full game and preorder bonus, including:

  • Up to five days of early access beginning July 23
  • The Alpha Halo Armory Pack
  • A Digital Story & Art Collection that includes Halo: Hungry Buzzards - Per Xbox Wire, it's "a new digital short story by Troy Denning that leads into the events of Operation: METEORITE"
  • The Art of Halo: Campaign Evolved digital artbook
  • A digital game manual inspired by the original 2001 Halo: Combat Evolved game manual

Below, you can get a closer look at the Masher Chief skins and weapon skins included in the Alpha Halo Armory Pack.

Halo: Campaign Evolved – Collector’s Edition

  • See at Xbox Game Studios Shop - $199.99 (CURRENTLY OUT OF STOCK)

The Halo: Campaign Evolved Collector's Edition looks like quite the pick-up. For $199.99, you get everything in the Premium Edition alongside:

  • 12-inch Dark Horse Master Chief statue
  • Light-up LED Cortana chip
  • A Steelbook
  • Three original concept art prints
  • Physical version of the reimagined game manual
  • A game disc for Xbox Series X or PlayStation 5 consoles (the Steam version includes a code only)

Halo: Campaign Evolved Preorder Bonus

Any edition of Halo: Campaign Evolved that you preorder will come with a bonus of the Foundry Armory Pack, which you can get a closer look at in the image above. This comes with:

  • Classic 2001 Mark V Armor skin
  • Classic 2001 Assault Rifle skin
  • Gilded Onyx Armor style
  • Gilded Onyx Assault Rifle style

Halo: Campaign Evolved is an Unreal Engine 5 remake of 2001's Halo: Combat Evolved. It features the original 10-mission campaign alongside something new that was revealed during the most recent Xbox Games Showcase: Operation: METEORITE.

According to an Xbox Wire post from Brian Jarrard, Community Director at Halo Studios, "Included with every edition of Halo: Campaign Evolved, Operation: METEORITE is a new three-mission story arc that takes place a year before the events of the first game. Featuring the Master Chief and Sgt. Johnson, this new adventure sends players behind enemy lines in a clandestine UNSC operation aboard a Covenant research vessel."

IGN's Ryan McCaffrey was also able to play a bit of Halo: Campaign Evolved last year and wrote in a preview, "I’m still left with many unanswered questions about this remake project. But to Halo Studios' credit, this early demo did answer a couple of key ones about how good it could look and how Halo-y it could feel."

More Preorder Guides

  • The Adventures of Elliot: The Millennium Tales Preorder Guide
  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Preorder Guide
  • Castlevania: Belmont's Curse Preorder Guide
  • Disney Afternoon Collection (Switch) Preorder Guide
  • Final Fantasy VII Rebirth (Switch 2) Preorder Guide
  • Forza Horizon 6 Preorder Guide
  • Indiana Jone and the Great Circle (Switch 2) Preorder Guide
  • LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Preorder Guide
  • Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls Preorder Guide
  • Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 2 Preorder Guide
  • Rhythm Heaven Groove Preorder Guide
  • Silent Hill: Townfall Preorder Guide
  • Splatoon Raiders Preorder Guide
  • Star Fox (Switch 2) Preorder Guide
  • Star Wars: Galactic Racer Preorder Guide
  • Yoshi and the Mysterious Book Preorder Guide

Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.

Recommends
